course_description:
This course will allow you to apply your knowledge of fundamental soil mechanics to the analysis and design of structures very commonly required in all civil infrastructure development from small and large buildings to road and highway construction, and wharf and port structures. The course covers the analysis of earth pressures, design of retaining walls, bearing capacity and settlement of shallow footings, design of pile footings and the slope stability of natural and man-made slopes.

This course continues the development of soil mechanics theory and its application to geotechnical analysis and design.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: The course aims to develop an understanding of the geotechnical design process and to appreciate the challenges and underlying complexity of the design of geotechnical infrastructure. The course will extend fundamental understanding of soil behaviour and apply these concepts to the design and analysis of retaining walls, shallow footings, deep footings and slopes.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the geotechnical design process and the challenges faced by geotechnical engineers in designing civil infrastructure.
Evaluate the lateral earth pressures at failure for various types of soils and groundwater conditions.
Explain how limit analysis methods are employed in designing geotechnical structures.
Apply elasticity theory to geotechnical design problems and understand their limitations.
Design gravity and embedded retaining walls in accordance with accepted engineering practice and standards.
Evaluate the ultimate bearing capacity and expected settlement of shallow footings.
Assess the axial and lateral capacity and deformation of deep footings.
Assess the stability of natural and man-made slopes.
Understand and describe basic soil behaviour related to geotechnical problems including retaining walls, shallow and deep footings, and slope stability while collaborating in a team.

assessment_summary.title: Course Content Quizzes
assessment_summary.due_date: Retaining Wall (5%) 27/03/2026 12:00 pm; Shallow Footing (5%) 1/05/2026 12:00 pm; Pile Foundations (5%) 15/05/2026 12:00 pm; Slope Stability (5%) 29/05/2026 12:00 pm; Quiz will be available for 24 hours (from Thursday 12:00 to Friday 12:00) to be completed within one (1) hour.

assessment_details.title: Laboratory Practical (Team)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will work in pairs to conduct one of five experiments during the session. Each pair will create a 7-minute video documenting their experiment, which will include an audio explanation of the objectives, methodology, and findings. Additionally, they will explain the other experiments they have not conducted themselves and answer pre-set questions related to both their experiment and the others. This assessment ensures a comprehensive understanding of their own work and the overall laboratory context. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission of video file using the provided link on Blackboard Ultra.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

Late submission
Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Contact Session - Discussion & Problem Solving The Contact Session is an opportunity for students to ask questions and receive feedback and additional information. These sessions will also feature guest lectures from practitioners and provide a platform for solving in-class problems. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.topic: Laboratory Practical A laboratory practical featuring five (5) experiments, each conducted by pairs of students, includes video documentation as an assessment component. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06, L09
